paper_title: Inferred from section titles: Histological and In Vivo Wound Healing Study

methods.description: This method describes the process for preparing and analyzing skin samples from a wound healing study using various histological staining techniques (Hematoxylin and Eosin, Masson's Trichrome, and Immunofluorescence for CD31) followed by microscopic observation and quantitative image analysis.

steps.description: The skin samples harvested from in vivo experiment were immersed in 4% paraformaldehyde for at least 24 h
steps.searchable_summary: fix biological tissue samples in paraformaldehyde
inputs: skin samples from in vivo experiment

steps.description: then dehydrated
steps.searchable_summary: dehydrate fixed tissue samples

steps.description: embedded in paraffin
steps.searchable_summary: embed dehydrated tissue samples in paraffin

steps.description: and cut into sections with thickness of 6 μm.
steps.searchable_summary: section paraffin-embedded tissue blocks using a microtome

steps.description: After drying, the sections were baked at 60°C for 1 h
steps.searchable_summary: dry and bake tissue sections onto slides

steps.description: followed by deparaffinizing to water
steps.searchable_summary: deparaffinize tissue sections and rehydrate

steps.description: and staining with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) (Sigma-Aldrich, USA)
steps.searchable_summary: stain tissue sections with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E)

steps.description: Masson's Trichrome (Beyotime, China)
steps.searchable_summary: stain tissue sections with Masson's Trichrome

steps.description: and immunofluorescence for histological analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform immunofluorescence staining on tissue sections

steps.description: For immunofluorescent CD31 protein staining, the sections were immersed in 0.01 M citrate buffer solution with pH of 6.0 at 99°C for 20 min for antigen retrieval.
steps.searchable_summary: perform antigen retrieval using citrate buffer for immunofluorescence

steps.description: After cooling, the nonspecific binding was blocked by dropping 5% (v/v) bovine serum albumin on the sections.
steps.searchable_summary: block non-specific binding sites on tissue sections using BSA

steps.description: Next, sliced tissues were incubated with a diluted (1:200) CD31 primary antibody (ab28364, Abcam) solution overnight at 4°C
steps.searchable_summary: incubate tissue sections with primary antibody (CD31)
outputs: tissue sections with primary antibody bound

steps.description: and then, solution of green fluorescent secondary antibody was dropped and incubated for 1 h in the dark.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate tissue sections with fluorescent secondary antibody
inputs: tissue sections with primary antibody bound

steps.description: The observation of stained sections was performed using a microscope (Model DMi8 S, Leica, Germany)
steps.searchable_summary: acquire images of stained tissue sections using a light microscope

steps.description: and a confocal laser scanning microscope (Model TCS SP8, Leica, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: acquire images of immunofluorescence stained tissue sections using a confocal microscope

steps.description: Moreover, the statistics of collagen area and vessel number were conducted using ImageJ (NIH, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: quantify collagen area from histological images using ImageJ

steps.description: Moreover, the statistics of collagen area and vessel number were conducted using ImageJ (NIH, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: quantify vessel number from histological images using ImageJ

methods.method_name: In Vivo Burn Wound Healing Study in Mice
methods.description: This method describes the procedure for establishing a second-degree burn wound model in BALB/c mice, applying different treatments (scaffolds), monitoring wound healing over time through imaging, and quantifying wound closure.

steps.description: The wound-healing animal experiment was proceeded according to the guidelines sanctified by the Institutional Animal Care and Utilization Committee of Nanjing First Hospital, Nanjing Medical University.
steps.searchable_summary: ensure animal experiments adhere to ethical guidelines and obtain approval

steps.description: BALB/c mice (8 weeks old, male, SPF grade) which purchased from the Charles River Laboratories Research Model Technology Co. Ltd. (Beijing, China) were used in this study.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire experimental animals (mice) from a supplier
outputs: BALB/c mice (8 weeks old, male, SPF grade)

steps.description: First, mice were anesthetized by intraperitoneal injection
steps.searchable_summary: anesthetize mice via intraperitoneal injection

steps.description: and the hair on the murine back was removed.
steps.searchable_summary: remove hair from the back of mice

steps.description: After disinfection, a metal rod (10 mm in diameter of section) immersed in boiling water at 100°C was pressed on the back of the mice for 5 s to create a deep burn wound with circular shape (10 mm in diameter).
steps.searchable_summary: create a second-degree burn wound on mouse back using a heated metal rod

steps.description: After that, the mice were split into three groups randomly: Blank, Gel, and 5DE-Gel.
steps.searchable_summary: randomly assign mice with wounds to experimental groups
outputs: mice assigned to experimental groups (Blank, Gel, 5DE-Gel)

steps.description: The 3D-printed scaffolds (5 mm of radius and 1 mm of thickness) were transplanted onto the burn wound site on murine back
steps.searchable_summary: transplant scaffolds onto the burn wound site (for Gel and 5DE-Gel groups)
outputs: mice with scaffolds transplanted on wounds

steps.description: and then medical dressings (3M, USA) were used to fix the scaffolds.
steps.searchable_summary: fix scaffolds/wounds using medical dressings
outputs: mice with treated and dressed wounds

steps.description: Next, the wounds were recorded by phone camera on days 0, 2, 5, 8, 11, and 24.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire images of wounds at specified time points using a camera
outputs: wound images at multiple time points

steps.description: The scaffolds were changed at each time point.
steps.searchable_summary: change scaffolds at each wound imaging time point

steps.description: The area of wound sites was measured using ImageJ (NIH, USA), an image processing software.
steps.searchable_summary: measure wound area from images using ImageJ

steps.description: The formula to calculate the relative wound area is as follows: Relative wound area (%) = A t /A _ 0 × 10 0 % Where, A t indicates the value of the wound bed area at some point in time A _ 0 and indicates the value of initial wound area (0 d).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative wound area percentage

steps.description: After 14 days, the skin samples were obtained after sacrificing all of the mice
steps.searchable_summary: sacrifice mice and collect skin samples from the wound site

steps.description: and then fixed in 4% paraformaldehyde.
steps.searchable_summary: fix collected skin samples in paraformaldehyde
